claim_ledger.claim: JFrog mapped 148 package names and 398 malicious package/version pairs.
May history contained a mutable loader and Wisp-compatible browser traffic generator.
An archived May 30 page generated HTTP flood traffic from visitors' browsers.
SafeDep's May 27 package snapshot had adware/static proxy assets and no install hook or credential stealer.
The July 8 wave currently executes DDoS code.
evidence: JFrog says the July builds were adware-only

claim_ledger.claim: 147 names now resolve to npm security placeholders; charlie-kirk 2.0.0/3.0.1 remained available by metadata.

source_conflict_resolution: SafeDep and JFrog inspected different evidence layers/times. SafeDep accurately described the published May package snapshot; JFrog later recovered earlier mutable behavior from history and archives.
